metadata.label: gen2/Fireblocks-SRE
metadata.title: Site Reliability Engineer
metadata.team_direction: Fireblocks is a leader in providing secure solutions for digital assets and blockchain technology

portfolio_review.title: Site Reliability Engineer
revision_priority: 补一条明确指向 Fireblocks 业务语境的安全/高可用基础设施叙事，避免简历看起来只是通用后端与平台经验。
在 DiDi 和 TikTok 的角色说明中再补 scope 限定语，降低“分析岗却承担工程 ownership”带来的可信度追问。

findings.issue: Opening with "Transitioning from data analysis to software engineering" makes the first screen read like a pivot story before it reads like an SRE/back-end hireable profile.
findings.fix: Reframe the first clause to lead with the target role identity and core infra stack, e.g. "Reliability-focused backend/SRE engineer with 3+ years building Python, Java, and Go services..." and move the transition context later or remove it.

findings.issue: The JD explicitly calls out configuration management / GitOps tools such as Ansible, Chef, Puppet, or ArgoCD, but the resume shows adjacent release and deployment work without naming any config-management tool. That leaves a small but real delivery-tooling gap for an SRE screen.
findings.fix: Add one bullet in the most relevant infra-heavy role that names the actual deployment/config tool used, such as an ArgoCD rollout, Ansible-driven config automation, or equivalent GitOps workflow, and tie it to the availability or release outcome.

revision_priority: [SHOULD] Rewrite the Summary opening so it leads with "Reliability-focused backend/SRE engineer" instead of the transition narrative.
[SHOULD] Add explicit config-management / GitOps evidence (ArgoCD or Ansible equivalent) in the most relevant infra-heavy experience bullet.
[SHOULD] If true, add one concrete reliability scale anchor to the newest experience so the SRE story reads as operating at material scope, not only local optimization.
